summary:
The patient’s symptom pattern—sharp, non-exertional chest pain worse at rest and relieved by leaning forward—with associated friction rub strongly suggests pericarditis. First-line treatment should therefore involve an NSAID combined with colchicine to manage inflammation and reduce risk of recurrence.  

\boxed{B} 

---

(End of Round)
